memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Moores_Ancestry
memoryArtifacts.description: This document was compiled by Orville Sullivan L6L2-JJB who is a grandson of Carrie Moore and Delos Sullivan, I was given a paper copy and decided to scan it in and create the searchable text form of the document so others may see and use it. I  tagged a number of the people found in the document to make it easier for others to locate.

memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Sullivan_Family_Sheet
memoryArtifacts.description:
This family sheet was found in papers of Mildred Evelyn Sullivan LNKH-8PV a granddaugher of Delos.

It was created by her brother Orville Oscar  Sullivan L6L2-JJB who had done a great deal of searching for ancestors. 

Many of the children of Delos appear to have had children.  None were entered at this time (7 July 2020) as I do not know their status

Nte that for Fay William the birth is Sept 21 not 2

zh.lifeSummary: 當Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham於1907年5月30日出生在美國明尼蘇達州時，她的父親Monte Lyman Cunningham30歲，母親Sarah S Reightley28歲。她和Oscar Cornelius Sullivan至少有7個兒子和2個女兒。她於1949年住在美國且於1950年住在美國華盛頓州可利吉塔特可利吉塔特。她去世於1975年1月30日享年67歲，埋葬在美國華盛頓州可利吉塔特懷特 賽門。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham于1907年5月30日出生在美国明尼苏达州时，她的父亲Monte Lyman Cunningham30岁，母亲Sarah S Reightley28岁。她和Oscar Cornelius Sullivan至少有7个儿子和2个女儿。她于1949年住在美国且于1950年住在美国华盛顿州可利吉塔特可利吉塔特。她去世于1975年1月30日享年67岁，埋葬在美国华盛顿州可利吉塔特懷特 賽門。

nameMeanings.name: Cunningham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from the province of Cunningham in Ayrshire, first recorded in 1153 in the form Cunegan, a Celtic name of uncertain origin. The spellings in -ham, first recorded in 1180, and in -ynghame, first recorded in 1227, represent a gradual assimilation to the English placename element -ingham.

Irish: surname adopted from Gaelic Ó Cuinneagáin ‘descendant of Cuinneagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of the Old Irish personal name Conn meaning ‘leader, chief’. This name is also adopted for Ó Connacháin, a variant of Ó Connagáin ‘descendant of Connagán’, from a diminutive of the personal name Conn.
History: A family of this name (see 1 above) can be traced back to Wernebald de Cunynghame, who was granted the manor of Cunningham by Hugh de Morville in the early 12th century.

variants.name: Cunigan
meanings: Altered form of Scottish or Irish   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Conahan
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Connachaín (see Cunningham ).

variants.name: Conaghan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Fergal.
Scottish (Lanarkshire and Renfrewshire) Irish (Donegal): from Ó Connacháin, a variant of Ó Connagáin, see Cunningham .

variants.name: Cuningham
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Cummingham
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of   Cunningham  .

variants.name: Cunnigham
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): variant of   Cunningham  .

nameMeanings.name: Gertrude
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic female personal name, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + þrūþ ‘strength’. The name is not found in England immediately after the Conquest, but only in the later Middle English period. It may have been introduced by migrants from the Low Countries who came to England in connection with the cloth trade, and was certainly in consistent use in some areas throughout the 16th and 17th centuries, although it was not generally popular until the 19th century, when many medieval names were revived. It has now fallen from favour again.

nameMeanings.name: Cecilia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

variants.name: Cecelia
meanings: From the Latin name Caecilia, feminine form of Caecilius ( see Cecil ). This was a good deal more common than the masculine form, largely due to the fame of the 2nd- or 3rd-century virgin martyr whose name is still mentioned daily in the Roman Catholic Canon of the Mass. She is regarded as the patron saint of music and has inspired works such as Purcell's ‘Ode on St Cecilia's Day’, although the reasons for this association are not clear.

historicEvents.title: The Bureau of Investigation is formed
historicEvents.description: Known as the National Bureau of Criminal Identification, The Bureau of Investigation helped agencies across the country identify different criminals. President Roosevelt instructed that there be an autonomous investigative service that would report only to the Attorney General.

historicEvents.title: Women Gain the Right to Vote in Washington
historicEvents.description: On November 8, 1910, Washington became the first state to allow women to vote. This event would lead to the long battle by women in all the states to fight for the right to vote. This would happen 10 years later.
Charles Lindbergh makes the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight in his plane The Spirit of St. Louis.

historicEvents.title: The Prehistoric Minnesota Woman
historicEvents.description: The Minnesota Woman was the name given to the skeletal remains of a woman thought to be 8,000 years old found near Pelican Rapids. The bones were brought to the University of Minnesota for more study. Later, Dr. Albert Jenks identified them as the bones of a 15 or 16 year old woman. Scientists now recognize the girl as someone whose ancestors were Paleo-Indian and now her skeletal remains have been reburied in South Dakota, not available for further study.

historicEvents.title: The Japanese American internment
historicEvents.description: Caused by the tensions between the United States and the Empire of Japan, the internment of Japanese Americans caused many to be forced out of their homes and forcibly relocated into concentration camps in the western states. More than 110,000 Japanese Americans were forced into these camps in fear that some of them were spies for Japan.

historicEvents.title: KING-TV
historicEvents.description: In 1948, KING-TV was the first TV station not just in Washington, but the whole Pacific Northwest. It is an NBC tv station licensed to Seattle and Tacoma.
historicEvents.urlTitle: History Link. Org-  KRSCC: Seattle's Radio and TV Pioneers

historicEvents.description: Squaw Valley, California, United States hosts Winter Olympic Games.

historicEvents.title: Seattle Space Needle is Built
historicEvents.description: Construction on the Seattle Space Needle was constructed on April 17, 1961, and was opened on April 21, 1962. It was built for the World’s Fair in 1962. The space needle is an observation tower that is iconic to Seattle and a landmark of the Pacific Northwest. It was meant to only be lite for 75 nights per year or less to reduce light pollution. On September 11, 2001 the lights of the space were left on for 11 days straight.

en.lifeSummary: When Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham was born on 30 May 1907, in Minnesota, United States, her father, Monte Lyman Cunningham, was 30 and her mother, Sarah S Reightley, was 28. She had at least 7 sons and 2 daughters with Oscar Cornelius Sullivan. She lived in United States in 1949 and Klickitat, Klickitat, Washington, United States in 1950. She died on 30 January 1975, at the age of 67, and was buried in White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham est née le 30 mai 1907, à Minnesota, États-Unis, son père, Monte Lyman Cunningham, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Sarah S Reightley, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles avec Oscar Cornelius Sullivan. Elle a vécu à États-Unis en 1949 et à Klickitat, Klickitat, Washington, États-Unis en 1950. Elle est décédée le 30 janvier 1975, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham am 30. Mai 1907 in Minnesota, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Monte Lyman Cunningham 30 und ihre Mutter Sarah S Reightley 28. Sie hatte mit Oscar Cornelius Sullivan mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1949 hat sie in Vereinigte Staaten und 1950 in Klickitat, Klickitat, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Sie starb am 30. Januar 1975 im Alter von 67 Jahren und wurde in White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham è nata il il 30 maggio 1907 nel Minnesota, Stati Uniti d'America, sua padre, Monte Lyman Cunningham, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Sarah S Reightley, aveva 28 anni. Lei avuto almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie con Oscar Cornelius Sullivan. Vissuto a Stati Uniti d'America nel 1949 e nel Klickitat, Klickitat, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1950. Morì il 30 gennaio 1975, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Gertrude Cecilia Cunninghamが1907年5月30日にアメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州で生まれた時，父Monte Lyman Cunninghamは30歳，母Sarah S Reightleyは28歳であった。彼女はOscar Cornelius Sullivanとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1949年にはアメリカ合衆国に，1950年にはアメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クリクイタトクリクイタトに住んでいた。彼女は1975年1月30日に67歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ワシントン州クリクイタトウィト サルモンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham(이)가 1907년 5월 30일 미국 미네소타 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Monte Lyman Cunningham(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah S Reightley(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Oscar Cornelius Sullivan(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1949년도에 미국에서, 1950년도에 미국 워싱턴 주 클릭이타트 클릭이타트에서 살았다. 그녀는 1975년 1월 30일 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 워싱턴 주 클릭이타트 휘트 살몬에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham nasceu em 30 de maio de 1907, em Minesota, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Monte Lyman Cunningham, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Sarah S Reightley, tinha 28 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas com Oscar Cornelius Sullivan. Ela viveu em Estados Unidos da América em 1949 e em Klickitat, Klickitat, Washington, Estados Unidos da América em 1950. Ela faleceu em 30 de janeiro de 1975, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em White Salmon, Klickitat, Washington, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gertrude Cecilia Cunningham родилась 30 мая 1907 г. в местности Миннесота, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Monte Lyman Cunningham было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah S Reightley, 28. У неё было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери от Oscar Cornelius Sullivan. Она жила в местности Соединенные штаты в 1949 году и в местности кликкитат, кликкитат, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты в 1950 году. Она умерла 30 января 1975 г. в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности уит салмон, кликкитат, Вашингтон, Соединенные штаты.
